author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.

ccel.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

source.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

title: Psalm LXXXIV

source.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This Psalm is entitled, “For the winepresses.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And, as you observed with me, my beloved (for I saw that you attended most closely), nothing is said in its text either of any press, or wine-basket, or vat, or of any of the instruments or the building of a winepress; nothing of this kind did we hear read; so that it is no easy question what is the meaning of this title inscribed upon it, “for the winepresses.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For certainly, if after the title it mentioned anything about such things as I enumerated, carnal persons might have believed that it was a song concerning those visible wine-presses; but as it has this title, yet says nothing afterwards of those winepresses which we know so well, I cannot doubt that there are other wine-presses, which the Spirit of God intended us to look for and to understand here.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore, let us recall to mind what takes place in these visible winepresses, and see how this takes place spiritually in the Church.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The grape hangs on the vines, and the olive on its trees.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For it is for these two fruits that presses are usually made ready; and as long as they hang on their boughs, they seem to enjoy free air; and neither is the grape wine, nor the olive oil, before they are pressed.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thus it is with men whom God predestined before the world to be conformed to the image of His only-begotten Son, who has been first and especially pressed in His Passion, as the great Cluster.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Men of this kind, therefore, before they draw near to the service of God, enjoy in the world a kind of delicious liberty, like hanging grapes or olives: but as it is said, “My son, when thou drawest near to the service of God, stand in judgment and fear, and make thy soul ready for temptation:” so each, as he draweth near to the service of God, findeth that he is come to the winepress; he shall undergo tribulation, shall be crushed, shall be pressed, not that he may perish in this world, but that he may flow down into the storehouses of God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He hath the coverings of carnal desires stripped off from him, like grape-skins: for this hath taken place in him in carnal desires, of which the Apostle speaks, “Put ye off the old man, and put on the new man.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: All this is not done but by pressure: therefore the Churches of God of this time are called winepresses.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But who are we who are placed in the wine-presses?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Sons of Core.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For this follows: “For the winepresses, to the sons of Core.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The sons of Core has been explained, sons of the bald: as far as those could explain it to us, who know that language, according to their service due to God.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But being placed under pressure, we are crushed for this purpose, that for our love by which we were borne towards those worldly, secular, temporal, unstable, and perishable things, having suffered in them, in this life, torments, and tribulations of pressures, and abundance of temptations, we may begin to seek that rest which is not of this life, nor of this earth; and the Lord becomes, as is written, “a refuge for the poor man.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is, “for the poor man”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For him who is, as it were, destitute, without aid, without help, without anything on which he may rest, in earth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For to such poor men, God is present.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For though men abound in money on earth,…they are filled more with fear than with enjoyment.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For what is so uncertain as a rolling thing?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It is not unfitly that money itself is stamped round, because it remains not still.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Such men, therefore, though they have something, are yet poor.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But those who have none of this wealth, but only desire it, are counted also among rich men who will be rejected; for God takes account not of power, but of will.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The poor then are destitute of all this world’s substance, for even though it abounds around them, they know how fleeting it is; and crying unto God, having nothing in this world with which they may delight themselves, and be held down, placed in abundant pressures and temptations, as if in winepresses, they flow down, having become oil or wine.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What are these latter but good desires?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For God remains their only object of desire; now they love not earth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For they love Him who made heaven and earth; they love Him, and are not yet with Him.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Their desire is delayed, in order that it may increase; it increases, in order that it may receive.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For it is not any little thing that God will give to him who desires, nor does he need to be little exercised to be made fit to receive so great a good: not anything which He hath made will God give, but Himself who made all things.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Exercise thyself to receive God: that which thou shalt have for ever, desire thou for a long time.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Wherefore, most beloved, as each can, make vows, and perform to the Lord God what each can: let no one look back, no one delight himself with his former interests, no one turn away from that which is before to that which is behind: let him run until he arrive: for we run not with the feet but with the desire.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But let no one in this life say that he hath arrived.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For who can be so perfect as Paul?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Yet he saith, “Brethren, I count not myself to have attained.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If therefore thou feelest the passions of this world, even when thou art happy, thou understandest now that thou art in the winepress.…If therefore the world smile upon thee with happiness, imagine thyself in the winepress, and say, “I found trouble and heaviness, and I did call upon the name of the Lord.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He said not, I found trouble, without meaning, of such a kind as was hidden: for some troubles are hidden from some in this world, who think they are happy while they are absent from God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For as long as we are in the body,” he saith, “we are absent from the Lord.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If thou wert absent from thy father, thou wouldest be unhappy: art thou absent from the Lord, and happy?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: There are then some who think it is well with them.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But those who understand, that in whatever abundance of wealth and pleasures, though all things obey their beck, though nothing troublesome creep in, nothing adverse terrify, yet that they are in a bad case as long as they are absent from the Lord; with a most keen eye these have found trouble, and grief, and have called on the name of the Lord.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Such is he who sings in this Psalm.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The Body of Christ.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Who is that?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: You, if you will: all we, if we will: for Christ’s Body is one.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “How lovely are Thy tabernacles, O Lord of Hosts” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He was in some tabernacles, that is, in winepresses: but he longed for other tabernacles, where is no pressure: in this he sighed for them, from these, he, as it were, flowed down into them by the channel of longing desire.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And what follows?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “My soul longeth and faileth for the courts of the Lord” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It is not enough that it “longeth and faileth:” for what doth it fail?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For the courts of the Lord.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The grape when pressed hath failed: but for what?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: So as to be changed into wine, and to flow into the vat, and into the rest of the storeroom, to be kept there in great quiet.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Here it is longed for, there it is received: here are sighs, there joy: here prayers, there praises: here groans, there rejoicing.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Those things which I mentioned, let no one while here turn from ashamed: let no one be unwilling to suffer.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: There is danger, lest the grape, while it fears the winepress, should be devoured by birds or by wild beasts.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou hast heard a groan in the winepress, “My soul longeth and faileth for the courts of the Lord:” hear how it holdeth out, rejoicing in hope: “My heart and my flesh have rejoiced in the living God.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Here they have rejoiced for that cause.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whence cometh rejoicing, but of hope?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Wherefore have they rejoiced?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “In the living God.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What has rejoiced in thee?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “My heart and my flesh.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Why have they rejoiced?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For,” saith he, “the sparrow hath found her a house, and the turtle-dove a nest, where she may lay her young” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is this?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He had named two things, and he adds two figures of birds which answer to them: he had said that his heart rejoiced and his flesh, and to these two he made the sparrow and turtle-dove to correspond: the heart as the sparrow, the flesh as the dove.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The sparrow hath found herself a home: my heart hath found itself a home.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: She tries her wings in the virtues of this life, in faith, and hope, and charity, by which she may fly unto her home: and when she shall have come thither, she shall remain; and now the complaining voice of the sparrow, which is here, shall no longer be there.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For it is the very complaining sparrow of whom in another Psalm he saith, “Like a sparrow alone on the housetop.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: From the housetop he flies home.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Now let him be on the housetop, treading on his carnal house: he shall have a heavenly house, a perpetual home: that sparrow shall make an end of his complaints.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But to the dove he hath given young, that is, to the flesh: “the dove hath found a nest, where she may lay her young.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The sparrow a home, the dove a nest, and a nest too where she may lay her young.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: A home is chosen as for ever, a nest is framed for a time: with the heart we think upon God, as if the sparrow flew to her home: with the flesh we do good works.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For ye see how many good works are done by the flesh of the saints; for by this we work the things we are commanded to work, by which we are helped in this life.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Break thy bread to the hungry, and bring the poor and roofless into thy house; and if thou see one naked, clothe him:” and other such things which are commanded us we work only through the flesh.…We speak, brethren, what ye know: how many seem to do good works without the Church? how many even Pagans feed the hungry, clothe the naked, receive the stranger, visit the sick, comfort the prisoner? how many do this?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The dove seems, as it were, to bring forth young: but finds not herself a nest.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How many works may heretics do not in the Church; they place not their young in a nest.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They shall be trampled on and crushed: they shall not be kept, shall not be guarded.…In that faith lay thy young: in that nest work thy works.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For what the nests are, what that nest is, follows at once.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Having said, And the dove hath found herself a nest, where she may lay her young; as if thou hadst asked, What nest?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Thy altars, O Lord of Hosts, my King and my God.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is, “My King and my God?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou who rulest me, who hast created me.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 8. …“Blessed are those who dwell in Thy house” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 4).…If thou hast thy own house, thou art poor; if God’s, thou art rich.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In thy own house thou wilt fear robbers; of the house of God, He is Himself the wall.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore “blessed are those who dwell in Thy house.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They possess the heavenly Jerusalem, without constraint, without pressure, without difference and division of boundaries; all have it, and each have all.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Great are those riches.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Brother crowdeth not brother: there is no want there.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Next, what will they do there?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For among men it is necessity which is the mother of all employments.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I have already said, in brief, brethren, run in your mind through any occupations, and see if it is not necessity alone which produces them.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Those very eminent arts which seem so powerful in giving help to others, the art of speaking in their defence or of medicine in healing, for these are the most excellent employments in this life; take away litigants, who is there for the advocate to help? take away wounds and diseases? what is there for the physician to cure?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And all those employments of ours which are required and done for our daily life, arise from necessity.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: To plough, to sow, to clear fallow ground, to sail; what is it which produces all these works, but necessity and want?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Take away hunger, thirst, nakedness; who has need of all these things?…For instance, the injunction, “Break thy bread to the hungry.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For whom could you break bread, if there were nobody hungry?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Take in the roofless poor into thy house.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What stranger is there to take in, where all live in their own country?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What sick person to visit, where they enjoy perpetual health?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What litigants to reconcile, where there is everlasting peace?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What dead to bury, where there is eternal life?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: None of those honourable actions which are common to all men will then be your employment, nor any of these good works; the young swallows will then fly out of their nest.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: You have said already what we shall have; “Those who dwell in Thy house are blessed.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Say now what they shall do, for I see not then any need to induce me to action.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Even what I am now saying and arguing springs from some need.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Will there be any such argument there to teach the ignorant, or remind the forgetful?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Or will the Gospel be read in that country where the Word of God Itself shall be contemplated?…“They shall be always praising Thee.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This shall be our whole duty, an unceasing Hallelujah.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Think not, my brethren, that there will be any weariness there: if ye are not able to endure long here in saying this, it is because some want draws you away from that enjoyment.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If what is not seen gives not so much joy here, if with so much eagerness under the pressure and weakness of the flesh we praise that which we believe, how shall we praise that which we see?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “When death shall be swallowed up in victory, when this mortal shall have put on immortality,” no one will say, “I have been standing a long time;” no one will say, “I have fasted a long time,” “I have watched a long time.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For there shall be great endurance, and our immortal bodies shall be sustained in contemplation of God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And if the word which we now dispense to you keeps your weak flesh standing so long, what will be the effect of that joy? how will it change us?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For we shall be like Him, since we shall see Him as He is.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Being made like Him, when shall we ever faint? what shall draw us off?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Brethren, we shall never be satiated with the praise of God, with the love of God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If love could fail, praise could fail.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But if love be eternal, as there will there be beauty inexhaustible, fear not lest thou be not able to praise for ever Him whom thou shalt be able to love for ever.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For this life let us sigh.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But how shall we come thither?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Happy is the man whose strength is in Thee” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He knew where he was, and that by reason of the frailty of his flesh he could not fly to that state of blessedness: he thought upon his own burden, as it is said elsewhere; “For the corruptible body weighs down the soul, and the earthly house depresses the understanding which has many thoughts.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The Spirit calls upward, the weight of the flesh calls back again downward: between the double effort to raise and to weigh down, a kind of struggle ensues: this struggle goes toward the pressure of the winepress.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Hear how the Apostle describes this same struggle of the winepress, for he was himself afflicted there, there he was pressed.…“Miserable man that I am: who shall deliver me from the body of this death?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The grace of God through Jesus Christ our Lord.”…“For I delight in the Law of God according to the inner man.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But what shall I do? how shall I fly? how shall I arrive thither?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I see another law in my members,” etc.…And as in the words of the Apostle, that difficulty and that almost inextricable struggle is alleviated by the addition, “The grace of God through Jesus Christ our Lord;” so here, when he sighed in the ardent longing for the house of God, and those praises of God, and when a kind of despair arose at the feeling of the burden of the body and the weight of the flesh, again he awoke to hope, and said (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 5), “Blessed is the man whose taking up is in Thee.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What then does God supply by His grace to him whom He taketh hold of to lead him on?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He goes on to say: “He hath placed steps in his heart.”…Where does it place steps?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “In his heart, in the valley of weeping” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: So here thou hast for a winepress the valley of weeping, the very pious tears in tribulation are the new wine of those that love.…They went forth “weeping,” he says, “casting their seed.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore, by the grace of God may upward steps be placed in thy heart.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Rise by loving.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Hence the Psalm “of degrees” is called.…“He hath placed steps of ascent to the place which He hath appointed” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Now we lament; whence proceed our lamentations, but from that place where the steps of our ascent are placed?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whence comes our lamentation, but from that cause wherefore the Apostle exclaimed that he was a wretched man, because he saw another law in his members, warring against the law in his mind?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And whence does this proceed?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: From the penalty of sin.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And we thought that we could easily be righteous as it were by our own strength, before we received the command; “but when the command came, sin revived; but I died,” saith the Apostle.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For a law was given to men, not such as could save them at once, but it was to show them in what severe sickness they were lying.…But when sin was made manifest by the law given, sin was but increased, for it is both sin, and against the Law; “Sin,” saith he, “taking occasion by the command, wrought in me all manner of concupiscence.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What does he mean by “taking occasion by the law”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Having received the command, men tried as by their own strength to obey it; conquered by lust, they became guilty of transgression of this very command also.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But what saith the Apostle?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Where sin abounded, grace hath much more abounded;” that is, the disease increased, the medicine became of more avail.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Accordingly, my brethren, did those five porches of Solomon, in the middle of which the pool lay, heal the sick at all?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The sick, says the Evangelist, lay in the five porches.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In the Gospel we have and read it.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Those five porches are the law in the five books of Moses.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For this cause the sick were brought forth from their houses that they might lie in the porches.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: So the law brought the sick men forth, but did not heal them: but by the blessing of God the water was disturbed, as by an Angel descending into it.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: At the sight of the water troubled, the one person who was able, descended and was healed.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: That water surrounded by the five porches, was the people of the Jews shut up in their law.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The Lord came and disturbed this people, so that He Himself was slain.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For if the Lord had not troubled the Jews by coming down to them, would He have been crucified?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: So that the troubled water signified the Passion of the Lord, which arose from His troubling the Jewish people.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The sick man who believeth in this Passion, like him who descended into the troubled water, is healed thereby.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He whom the Law could not heal, that is, while he lay in the porches, is healed by grace, by faith in the Passion of our Lord Jesus Christ.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “He shall give blessing,” saith he, “who gave the law.”…Grace shall come after the law, grace itself is the blessing.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And what has that grace and blessing given unto us?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “They shall go from virtue to virtue.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For here by grace many virtues are given.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For to one is given by the Spirit the word of wisdom, to another the word of knowledge according to the same Spirit, to another faith, to another the gift of healing, to another different kinds of tongues, to another the interpretation of tongues, to another prophecy.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Many virtues, but necessary for this life; and from these virtues we go on to “a virtue.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: To what “virtue”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: To “Christ the Virtue of God and the Wisdom of God.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He giveth different virtues in this place, who for all the virtues which are necessary and useful in this valley of weeping shall give one virtue, Himself.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For in Scripture and in many writers four virtues are described useful for life: prudence, by which we discern between good and evil; justice, by which we give each person his due, “owing no man anything,” but loving all men: temperance, by which we restrain lusts; fortitude, by which we bear all troubles.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: These virtues are now by the grace of God given unto us in the valley of weeping: from these virtues we mount unto that other virtue.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And what will that be, but the virtue of the contemplation of God alone?…It follows in that place: “They shall go from virtue to virtue.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What virtue?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: That of contemplation.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is contemplation?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The God of Gods shall appear in Sion.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The God of Gods, Christ of the Christians.…When all is finished, that mortality makes necessary, He shall appear to the pure in heart, as He is, “God with God,” The Word with the Father, “by which all things were made.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And again, from the thought of those joys he returns to his own sighs.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He sees what has come before in hope, and where he is in reality.…Therefore returning to the groans proper to this place, he saith, “O Lord God of virtues, hear my prayer: hearken, O God of Jacob” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 8): for Jacob himself also Thou hast made Israel out of Jacob.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For God appeared unto him, and he was called Israel, seeing God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Hear me therefore, O God of Jacob, and make me Israel.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When shall I become Israel?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When the God of Gods shall appear in Sion.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Behold, O God our defender.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And look on the face of Thy Christ” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For when doth God not look upon the face of His Christ?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is this, “Look on the face of Thy Christ”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: By the face we are known.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is it then, Look on the face of Thy Christ?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Cause Thy Christ to become known to all.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Look on the face of Thy Christ: let Christ become known to all, that we may be able to go from strength to strength, that grace may abound, since sin hath abounded.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For one day in Thy courts is better than a thousand” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Those courts they were for which he sighed, for which he fainted.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “My soul longeth and faileth for the courts of the Lord:” one day there is better than a thousand days.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Men long for thousands of days, and wish to live here long: let them despise these thousands of days, let them long for one day, which has neither rising nor setting: one day, an everlasting day, to which no yesterday yields, which no to-morrow presses.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let this one day be longed for by us.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What have we to do with a thousand days?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: We go from the thousand days to one day; let us hasten to that one day, as we go from strength to strength.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I have chosen to be cast away in the house of the Lord, rather than to dwell in the tents of sinners” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For he found the valley of weeping, he found humility by which he might rise: he knoweth that if he would raise himself he shall fall, if he humble himself he shall be exalted: he hath chosen to be cast away, that he may be raised up.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How many beside this tabernacle of the Lord’s winepress, that is beside the Catholic Church, wishing to be lifted up, and loving their honours, refuse to see the truth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If this verse had been in their heart, would they not cast away honours, and run to the valley of weeping, and hence find in their heart the way of ascent, and hence go from virtues to virtue, placing their hope in Christ, not in some man or another?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: A good word is this, a word to rejoice in, a word to be chosen.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He himself chose to be cast away in the house of the Lord; but He who invited him to the feast, when he chose a lower place calleth him to a higher one, and saith unto him, “Go up higher.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Yet he chose not but to be in the house of the Lord, in any part of it, so that he were not outside the threshold.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Wherefore did he choose?…“Because God loveth mercy and truth” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The Lord loveth mercy, by which He first came to my help: He loveth truth, so as to give to him that believeth what He has promised.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Hear in the case of the Apostle Paul, His mercy and truth, Paul who was first Saul the persecutor.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He needed mercy, and he has said that it was shown towards him: “I who was before a blasphemer, and a persecutor, and injurious: but I obtained mercy, that in me Christ Jesus might show forth all longsuffering towards those who shall believe in Him unto life eternal.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: So that, when Paul received pardon of such great crimes, no one should despair of any sins whatever being forgiven him.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou hast Mercy.…Lo, we see that Paul holdeth Him a debtor, having received mercy, demanding truth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The Lord, he says, shall give back in that day.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What shall He give thee back, but that which He oweth thee?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How oweth He unto thee?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What hast thou given Him?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Who hath first given unto Him, and it shall be restored to him again.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The Lord Himself hath made Himself a debtor, not by receiving, but by promising: it is not said unto Him, Restore what Thou hast received: but, Restore what Thou hast promised.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He hath shown mercy unto me, he saith, that He might make me innocent: for before I was a blasphemer and injurious: but by His grace I have been made innocent.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But He who first showed mercy, can He deny His debt?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “He loveth mercy and truth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He will give grace and glory.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What grace, but that of which the same one said: “By the grace of God I am what I am”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What glory, but that of which he said, “There is laid up for me a crown of glory”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore “the Lord will not withhold good from those who walk in innocence” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Why then, O men, are ye unwilling to keep innocence, except in order that ye may have good things?…Thou seest wealth in the hands of robbers, of the impious, the wicked, the base; in the hands of scandalous and criminal men thou seest wealth: God giveth them these things on account of their fellowship in the human race, for the abundant overflowing of His goodness: who also “maketh His sun to rise upon the good and the evil, and causeth it to rain upon the righteous and upon the sinners.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Giveth He so much to the wicked, and keepeth nothing for thee?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He keepeth something: be at ease, He who had mercy on thee when thou wast impious, doth He desert thee when thou hast become pious?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He who gave to the sinner the free gift of His Son’s death, what keepeth He for the saved through that death?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore be at ease.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Hold Him a debtor, for thou hast believed in Him promising.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What then remains for us here, in the winepress, in affliction, in hardship, in our present dangerous life?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What remains for us, that we may arrive thither?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “O Lord God of virtues, blessed is the man that putteth his hope in Thee.”
